articles.title: Inowize Expands QBIX Play Immersive Gaming Rooms to FEC Venues Worldwide
articles.summary: Inowize is celebrating global adoption of QBIX Play, a 6-player immersive gaming room designed specifically for FECs, wristband venues, and party locations. The system fits in just 169 square feet, requires no dedicated staff, and features nine themed titles with sessions under five minutes — a low-barrier upsell for operators. A limited summer demo offer is available for FEC operators exploring the platform.

articles.title: Intercard Cashless Technology Installed at New Sports-Anchor FEC in Illinois
articles.summary: Intercard has been selected to power the arcade at Legends Entertainment's 46,000-square-foot facility inside Scheels Sports Park in Springfield, Illinois, a mixed-use development targeting sports tournament visitors. The deployment highlights the growing trend of sports-anchored FECs using cashless card systems to capture tournament traffic. For operators, this is a strong case study in pairing cashless tech with a sports tourism revenue stream.

articles.title: Universal Kids Resort Opens in Frisco, Texas — A New Benchmark for Family Entertainment
articles.summary: Universal Kids Resort has officially opened in Frisco, Texas, featuring seven themed areas built around beloved children's IP after three years of development. The resort represents a major new competitive entrant in the family entertainment space in a high-growth suburban market. FEC operators in the Dallas-Fort Worth region should take note of the increased competition and the guest experience bar this sets for immersive, IP-driven family attractions.

articles.title: The Breakers Resort Plans Upscale Family Entertainment Center in Palm Beach
articles.summary: Renderings have been released for a planned Family Entertainment Center at The Breakers, the iconic Palm Beach luxury resort, signaling growing interest in FEC concepts within premium hospitality settings. The project reflects a broader trend of hotels and resorts integrating entertainment amenities to drive ancillary revenue and guest retention. FEC designers and suppliers may find opportunity in the upscale resort FEC segment.

articles.title: $50M EVO Entertainment Destination Breaks Ground in Downtown Clearwater
articles.summary: A site has been cleared for a $50 million EVO Entertainment destination in Downtown Clearwater, Florida, signaling continued investment in large-format entertainment complexes in urban cores. EVO Entertainment is known for combining bowling, gaming, movies, and dining under one roof, making this a significant development for the multi-attraction FEC model. Operators and investors should monitor this project as a barometer of confidence in the entertainment destination format.

articles.title: New Waterpark and Entertainment Complex Planned for West Des Moines
articles.summary: New plans have been revealed for a waterpark and entertainment complex in West Des Moines, pointing to continued suburban market investment in large-scale family entertainment destinations. The project adds to a pipeline of mixed-use entertainment developments that combine aquatic attractions with broader FEC offerings. Regional FEC operators should watch this development for competitive implications and potential supplier opportunities.

articles.title: LEVEL 99 Expanding to Connecticut with First West Hartford Location
articles.summary: LEVEL 99, the activity-based entertainment concept known for its physical challenge courses and competitive gaming experiences, is opening its first Connecticut location in West Hartford. The expansion continues LEVEL 99's growth beyond its Massachusetts origin and signals strong consumer demand for skill-based, competitive entertainment formats. FEC operators should study this concept as a model for engagement-driven, non-traditional arcade experiences.

articles.title: Sky Zone Trampoline Park Opens New Location, Creates Local Jobs
articles.summary: A new Sky Zone Trampoline Park has opened, bringing family entertainment options and employment to the local community. Sky Zone continues its national expansion, reinforcing trampoline parks as a resilient FEC category. For operators considering trampoline-anchored concepts, the opening provides a current market signal on franchise viability.

articles.title: Altitude Trampoline Park to Open New Arizona Location in Litchfield Park
articles.summary: Altitude Trampoline Park is set to open a new location in Litchfield Park, Arizona, continuing the brand's expansion in Sun Belt markets. The opening reflects ongoing consumer appetite for active, family-focused indoor entertainment, particularly in high-growth suburban areas. Competitors and suppliers in the trampoline park space should note the continued footprint growth of major chains.

articles.title: Spare Time Texas Positions Itself as Affordable Indoor Entertainment Hub in Pflugerville
articles.summary: Spare Time Texas in Pflugerville is being highlighted as a go-to affordable indoor entertainment destination, combining bowling, arcade games, and food and beverage in a single venue. The affordability angle is increasingly important as operators navigate 'funflation' concerns and price-sensitive families. This profile offers a useful marketing case study for FEC operators looking to communicate value to guests.

articles.title: 'Funflation' Trend Hits Entertainment and Leisure Sectors as Costs Surge
articles.summary: A growing 'funflation' trend is pushing up the cost of leisure and entertainment activities including gaming and indoor attractions, creating affordability pressure for consumers and operators alike. Rising costs for labor, equipment, and real estate are squeezing FEC margins while guests become increasingly value-conscious. Operators should consider how pricing strategy and perceived value can be balanced to retain visitation frequency.

articles.title: Funflation Driving Up Indoor Leisure Costs, Raising Consumer Sensitivity
articles.summary: Indoor leisure costs are rising sharply, with funflation affecting everything from trampoline parks to arcades and bowling alleys. The trend puts FEC operators in a difficult position as guests push back against higher prices while operating costs continue to climb. Understanding this dynamic is critical for operators developing 2026 and 2027 pricing and promotional strategies.

articles.title: Eternity Bowling to Showcase EV99 String Bowling System at Fun Asia Expo 2026
articles.summary: Eternity Bowling will exhibit its EV99 String Bowling System at Fun Asia Expo 2026 in Indonesia, offering a look at string pin technology gaining traction in Asian FEC markets. String bowling systems are increasingly popular for their lower maintenance requirements and suitability for smaller venues and family audiences. FEC operators and bowling center owners considering equipment upgrades should follow this product's international momentum.

articles.title: Lefty's Alley and Eats Opens New Bowling and Dining Concept in Newark, Delaware
articles.summary: Lefty's Alley and Eats has opened a new location in Newark, Delaware, combining bowling lanes with a food and beverage experience in a format popular with modern FEC operators. The opening adds to the growing inventory of eatertainment concepts that blend dining with active recreation. Operators and developers can look to this opening as a local-market example of the bowling-plus-dining model in action.

articles.title: GTI China Expo 2026 Expands to 100,000 Square Meters, Covering Full Attractions Spectrum
articles.summary: The 18th GTI China Expo will take place September 10-12 in Guangzhou, expanding its exhibition floor to over 100,000 square meters — a 25% increase from 2025. The show will cover more than 60 industry segments including amusement rides, video games, VR/AR/MR, children's entertainment, and water park equipment. FEC operators and suppliers sourcing from Asia or tracking global product trends should consider attending or monitoring this key procurement event.

articles.title: IAAPA Expo Europe 2026 Earns Certified Sustainable Event Status
articles.summary: IAAPA Expo Europe 2026 has been certified as a sustainable event following an independent third-party review, achieving a 76% score against mandatory sustainability requirements. This certification reflects growing pressure on the attractions and entertainment industry to demonstrate environmental responsibility at major trade events. FEC operators and suppliers attending IAAPA Expo Europe can expect sustainability-focused exhibitor practices and programming.

articles.title: Boxing Arcade Machines Gaining Traction as Entertainment Venue Revenue Drivers
articles.summary: Boxing arcade machines are being positioned as a rising attraction category in entertainment venues, offering a physical, competitive experience that appeals to a broad demographic. The category benefits from low footprint requirements and high per-play revenue potential relative to traditional video arcade games. FEC operators looking to diversify their arcade floor should evaluate the current boxing machine market for suitable product options.

articles.title: InfoComm 2026: AV Industry Prioritizes Simplicity and Ease of Operation Over Raw Specs
articles.summary: At InfoComm 2026 in Las Vegas, the audiovisual industry signaled a shift toward systems that are easier to deploy, operate, and maintain — a welcome trend for FEC operators who often lack dedicated AV technical staff. Simplicity in media servers, projection, and display systems reduces operating costs and staff training requirements for entertainment venues. FEC operators investing in projection mapping, digital signage, or immersive experiences should factor ease-of-use into equipment decisions.

articles.title: Fiesta Village FEC in Colton, California Closes After Decades of Operation
articles.summary: Fiesta Village, a long-standing family entertainment center in Colton, California, has drawn large farewell crowds as it closes its doors for good. The closure offers a reminder of the ongoing challenges independent FECs face in a competitive and cost-intensive environment. For industry observers, the closure signals the importance of reinvestment and differentiation in sustaining legacy FEC properties.

articles.title: Developer Behind Proposed $3B Romulus Sports and Entertainment Complex Charged with Fraud
articles.summary: The developer behind a proposed $3 billion sports and entertainment complex in Romulus, Michigan has been charged with fraud, raising red flags about the project's viability. The case underscores the due diligence challenges investors and municipalities face when evaluating large-scale entertainment destination proposals. Industry stakeholders should treat this as a cautionary tale when assessing ambitious mixed-use entertainment development pitches.

articles.title: Teen Fatally Shot Outside Orlando's Dezerland Park, Raising Venue Safety Concerns
articles.summary: A 16-year-old has been arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of a 14-year-old outside Dezerland Park, an entertainment complex in Orlando. The incident highlights the importance of perimeter security planning and crowd management protocols for large entertainment venues. FEC operators should review their outdoor security procedures and coordination with local law enforcement in light of this tragedy.

articles.title: Philippine Authorities Raid Makati Arcade Operating as Illegal Gambling Den
articles.summary: The National Bureau of Investigation raided a Makati arcade where operators were allegedly running an illegal gambling scheme, offering empty prize boxes to players. The case is a reminder of regulatory and reputational risks associated with prize redemption and amusement operations in markets with unclear gaming laws. FEC operators internationally should ensure their redemption and prize game operations comply with local gambling regulations.

articles.title: PortAventura World Adding Two New Family-Focused Zones This Summer
articles.summary: PortAventura World in Spain is expanding its park offerings with two new zones designed for families, opening later this summer. The additions reflect continued investment by major European theme parks in family-targeted experiences to broaden their demographic appeal. FEC designers and operators can draw inspiration from the park's approach to family-zone development and theming.

articles.title: Dippin' Dots Marks National Ice Cream Day with Free Giveaways at FEC and Franchise Locations
articles.summary: Dippin' Dots is offering free mini cups at all 128 franchise locations nationwide on National Ice Cream Day, July 19, along with a chance to win free product for a year. For FEC operators who carry Dippin' Dots as a concession item, the promotion can drive incremental traffic and give staff a built-in guest engagement moment. This is a useful example of a concession brand driving footfall for host venues through national promotions.

articles.title: On The Stage Offers All-in-One Ticketing and Venue Management for Live Entertainment Operators
articles.summary: On The Stage is an integrated ticketing, box office, and venue management platform built for performing arts and live entertainment venues, offering tools for event websites, seating management, fundraising, and patron analytics from a single dashboard. For FEC operators running escape rooms, live shows, laser tag, or ticketed events, the platform could reduce administrative overhead and consolidate revenue management. The platform's accessibility without enterprise-level costs makes it worth evaluating for mid-size entertainment operators.

articles.title: Michigan's Adventure Celebrates 70th Anniversary with Free Admission for Guests 70 and Older
articles.summary: Michigan's Adventure is marking its 70th season by offering complimentary weekday admission to guests aged 70 and older through its new 70 Plus Club promotion. The campaign is a creative anniversary marketing tactic that generates goodwill, media attention, and multigenerational visitation. FEC operators can take note of milestone anniversary promotions as a tool for community engagement and incremental traffic.
